pages.raw_text:
Though suffering intense pain
from a broken hip, sustained in a
tumble down steep embankment,
Charles Deyo, a Great Northern
brakeman, dragged himself back
up the incline to switch and
saved the Oriental Limited from
disastrous wreck at Dryden. Wash,
last Sunday night. Deyo is now in
the Sacred Heart hospital taking
treatment for his injuries.


His train took the switch at
Dryden at rather rapid rate in
order to clear the track in time for
the speeding passenger train COM
ing only short way ahead. AS
Deyo. the rear brakeman of the
freight. lowered himself from the
caboose steps to turn the switch,
he lost his footing and was fairly
hurled over the rocks and hard
ground, to the foot of the embank-
ment on which the tracks are
built.


His work of struggling with his
paining. helpless limb dragging be-
hind him up the bank to the switch
and turning that to give the pas
senger train clear track is con-
sidered a very heroic deed. at least
1t is figured that many lives on
the limited would have been snuff
ed out and the train would likely
have been piled up in the ditch
had not Deyo reached the switch
in time.
